main_conclusion: The method should increasingly be framed as budget-conditioned selective marginal allocation rather than ordinary binary pair classification.
core_shift: Predict budget-conditioned branch-level residual or marginal continuation value and derive compare/defer decisions from value separation and uncertainty rather than supervising primarily on hard pairwise wins.
important_points: per-branch value is more faithful than direct pairwise winner labels
pairwise decisions should increasingly be derived rather than primary
hard ambiguous cases should not always be forced into total orders
raw value-gap magnitude is not automatically confidence
existing label pools should be reused more selectively before broad expansion
best_training_ideas: value head plus uncertainty or defer head
practical_experiment_ideas: branch-level value-head experiment with derived pairwise/defer decisions
relaxed pairwise target experiment on low-margin and disagreement pairs
safe_wording: The most promising next target-design direction is to model next-step branch allocation as budget-conditioned selective marginal allocation, using branch-level continuation-value signals, softer or structured supervision on ambiguous pairs, and explicit defer or unresolved handling rather than forcing all hard comparisons into binary winner labels.
not_yet_safe: branch-level value supervision is already the settled empirical winner
abstention or partial-order formulations are already closed
raw predicted value-gap magnitude is already a calibrated confidence measure
recommended_next_step: Run a bounded experiment on branch-level budget-conditioned value prediction with derived compare/defer, relaxed pairwise targets tied to provenance/disagreement metadata, or partial-order/unresolved treatment on hardest slices.
